L1.area: Cloud infrastructure management and controller API facade layer

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where any authenticated user with basic login permission to a controller can retrieve sensitive cloud infrastructure credentials by calling an API endpoint with a known model identifier. The endpoint does not verify that the caller has elevated privileges such as superuser or model admin permissions before returning confidential credential details, allowing unauthorized access to bootstrap credentials.

L3.area: Cloud specification credential retrieval within the shared API server components
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where any authenticated user with basic login permission to a controller can retrieve sensitive cloud infrastructure credentials by calling the cloud specification API endpoint with a known controller model UUID. The endpoint does not verify that the caller has superuser or model admin permissions before returning confidential credential details. Only controller or model agents should receive credentials, while client callers need superuser or model admin access. The vulnerability affects both VM and Kubernetes controller deployments.

ground_truth.reason: The cloud specification API endpoint returns cloud credentials to any authenticated client without verifying that the caller has superuser or model admin privileges, allowing unauthorized credential disclosure.
